_note: Branch-specific VSO contacts, VA regional offices, and benefit notes. Used to personalize chat responses and the output package for each veteran's branch of service.

Army.va_regional_note: Army veterans can locate their nearest VA Regional Office at va.gov/find-locations

branch_specific_benefits.name: Combat-Related Special Compensation (CRSC)
branch_specific_benefits.description: Army veterans with combat-related disabilities may receive CRSC in addition to VA disability pay. This is separate from standard VA compensation.

branch_specific_benefits.name: Traumatic Servicemembers Group Life Insurance (TSGLI)
branch_specific_benefits.description: If you suffered a qualifying traumatic injury during service, you may be eligible for a one-time TSGLI payment.

Army.greeting_note: Thank you for your service and sacrifice — and to your family for theirs as well.

Marine Corps.va_regional_note: Marine Corps veterans can locate their nearest VA Regional Office at va.gov/find-locations

branch_specific_benefits.name: Camp Lejeune Family Member Program
branch_specific_benefits.description: Veterans and family members who lived or worked at Camp Lejeune between August 1953 and December 1987 may be eligible for VA health care due to contaminated water exposure.

branch_specific_benefits.name: Combat-Related Special Compensation (CRSC)
branch_specific_benefits.description: Marine veterans with combat-related disabilities may qualify for CRSC payments in addition to standard VA disability compensation.

Marine Corps.greeting_note: Semper Fidelis — thank you for your service and dedication, and to your family who served alongside you.

Air Force.va_regional_note: Air Force veterans can locate their nearest VA Regional Office at va.gov/find-locations

branch_specific_benefits.name: Burn Pit / Airborne Hazards Exposure
branch_specific_benefits.description: Air Force veterans deployed near burn pits or exposed to airborne hazards may qualify for PACT Act benefits, including expanded VA health care and disability compensation.

branch_specific_benefits.name: Special Survivor Indemnity Allowance (SSIA)
branch_specific_benefits.description: Surviving spouses of Air Force veterans who receive Dependency and Indemnity Compensation may qualify for SSIA.

Air Force.greeting_note: Thank you for your service and sacrifice in the Air Force — and to your family for everything they gave as well.

Navy.va_regional_note: Navy veterans can locate their nearest VA Regional Office at va.gov/find-locations

branch_specific_benefits.name: Asbestos Exposure Benefits
branch_specific_benefits.description: Navy veterans — especially those who served on ships before the 1980s — have high rates of asbestos exposure and may qualify for related VA disability compensation.

branch_specific_benefits.name: Burn Pit / Airborne Hazards (PACT Act)
branch_specific_benefits.description: Navy veterans deployed near burn pits may qualify for expanded health care and compensation under the PACT Act.

Navy.greeting_note: Thank you for your service and the sacrifices you and your family made in the United States Navy.

Coast Guard.va_regional_note: Coast Guard veterans can locate their nearest VA Regional Office at va.gov/find-locations

branch_specific_benefits.name: Search and Rescue Operational Stress
branch_specific_benefits.description: Coast Guard veterans involved in search and rescue operations may have elevated PTSD and stress-related conditions that qualify for VA mental health benefits.

Coast Guard.greeting_note: Semper Paratus — thank you for your service in the Coast Guard and for everything you and your family gave.

default.va_regional_note: Find your nearest VA Regional Office at va.gov/find-locations
default.greeting_note: Thank you for your service and sacrifice — and to your family for theirs as well.
